title: Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) and Extended Childcare Leave (ECL)
text:
Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) and Extended Childcare Leave (ECL) - SupportGoWhere
Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) and Extended Childcare Leave (ECL)
Share link
Provides up to 6 days of leave for eligible working parents and legal guardians with children below 7 years old, and 2 days for those with children aged 7 to 12.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Up to 6 days of Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) for parents with a Singapore citizen child below 7 years old, capped at $500 per day for the 4th to 6th day, up to $1,500 per year
2 days of Extended Childcare Leave (ECL) for parents with a Singapore citizen child aged 7 to 12, capped at $500 per day, up to $1,000 per year
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

For parents with a Singapore citizen child below 7 years old:

Up to 6 days of Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL)

Employer pays for the first 3 days of GPCL

Government reimburses for the 4th to 6th day, capped at $500 per day, up to $1,500 per year

For parents with a Singapore citizen child between 7 and 12 years old:

2 days of Extended Childcare Leave (ECL)

Fully reimbursed by the Government, capped at $500 per day, up to $1,000 per year.

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizen child below 7 years old for GPCL or obtains Singapore citizenship within the calendar year

Singapore Citizen child aged 7 to 12 years old for ECL or obtains Singapore citizenship within the calendar year

Parent has served employer for at least 3 months or been self-employed for at least 3 months and have lost income from ceasing to be actively engaged during your GPCL/ECL period.

Adoptive parents, eligible only after Adoption Order is passed

Natural fathers not eligible if not lawfully married to the child's mother at conception and not married subsequently

Foster parents eligible until they cease providing care, protection or supervision

Legal guardians with court-appointed guardianship eligible

How to apply?

For employees:

Inform your employer of your leave plans.

For foster parents, show your Letter of Identity to your employer as proof that a foster child has been placed under your care under the Children and Young Persons Act. Your employer can write in to the MSF Fostering Service at fostering@msf.gov.sg to verify your eligibility for GPCL/ECL

For legal guardians, provide a copy of your Legal Guardianship document (i.e. Order of Court) to your employer, for verification on your GPCL/ECL eligibility.

Submit a declaration form (GPCL1) with necessary documents.

Apply for leave as per your employer's procedures.

For self-employed:

Record your leave dates.

Submit your claim on the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal within 3 months after the calendar year ends.

For employers:

Collect and verify the declaration form (GPCL1) from your employee.

Submit your reimbursement claim on the GPL Portal within 3 months after the 12-month period ends.

Where can I find help?

View more details

MSFCares: 1800 111 2222 
(say ‘GPLS’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)

Operating hours: Mon to Fri 8.30am to 6pm | Saturday: 8.30am to 1pm
(Closed on Public Holidays)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 15 Dec 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
